spec_match.rationale: The code sample now uses the numeric navigator.cpuPerformance attribute rather than the obsolete callable API.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP clicked every preset and moved a tier slider; histogram percentages and rollout guidance updated.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index and page both frame the simulator as audience planning for tier-gated paths.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now cites CrUX BigQuery and Device Memory bucketing background, but the preset values are still illustrative priors rather than a checked-in exported dataset.

references.rationale: Cites ChromeStatus plus public Chrome documentation for CrUX BigQuery and Device Memory bucketing.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout and no raw hex literals were found.

openQuestions.title: Replace illustrative preset values with an exported citable dataset
openQuestions.detail: The page now links to CrUX BigQuery and Device Memory bucketing background, but its US / EU / India / Brazil / Enterprise / Kiosk tier shares are still hand-authored priors. Import or document a checked-in query/export from real RUM before using these numbers for planning.

summary: Market presets, histogram editing, and rollout math are interactive and now cite public data sources, but the exact preset percentages still need a real exported dataset to be fully defensible.
